description: Apply a label to one or more emails in Gmail. Opens the email or selects emails in inbox, then applies the specified label via the label dropdown.
author: stillwater

reasoning.research: Gmail labels are applied via the Labels button in the toolbar (div[aria-label='Labels']). Clicking it opens a dropdown with div[role='menuitemcheckbox'] items. Labels can be searched/filtered with input[aria-label='Label as']. The Apply button confirms the selection. Labels can be applied from inbox (select emails first) or from within a thread view.
reasoning.selector_strategy: Use aria-label selectors for label button and search input. Use role='menuitemcheckbox' for label options in dropdown.
reasoning.anti_bot_notes: Low bot detection risk — label operations are UI click actions with no sensitive text input.
reasoning.performance: Navigation 3s + open email or select 1s + label dropdown 1s + apply 1s. Typical: 8-12s.

email_index.description: Index of email to label (0 = latest). Default 0.

steps.description: Click target email to open thread view
Wait for thread view to load
Click Labels button in toolbar to open label dropdown
Wait for label search input in dropdown
Type label name to filter dropdown options
Wait for dropdown filter to update
Select the matching label from dropdown
Click Apply to confirm label assignment

error_handling.label_not_found: If label does not exist in dropdown, create new label via 'Create new' option
error_handling.label_dropdown_timeout: Retry click on Labels button once, then abort
error_handling.session_expired: Detect accounts.google.com redirect — abort with SESSION_EXPIRED
